min_actual.description: Minimum guest memory target in MiB.

max_actual.description: Maximum guest memory target in MiB.

grow_below_available.description: Grow guest memory when available guest memory falls below this MiB threshold.

reclaim_above_available.description: Reclaim guest memory when available guest memory rises above this MiB threshold.

step.description: Memory adjustment step size in MiB.

poll_interval_seconds.description: Seconds between balloon controller polling cycles.

reclaim_holdoff_seconds.description: Seconds to wait after growing memory before reclaiming again.

enabled.description: Enable the virtio memory balloon device.

deflate_on_oom.description: Allow the guest to deflate the balloon under out-of-memory pressure.

free_page_reporting.description: Enable free page reporting for the balloon device.

controller.description: Optional host-side balloon controller thresholds and polling settings.

proto.description: Transport protocol for the forwarded port.

host.description: Host endpoint address in address:port form.

guest.description: Guest endpoint address in address:port form.

backend.description: Display backend; headless disables graphical output.

socket.description: Virtiofs daemon socket path relative to the runtime state directory unless absolute.

bin.description: Virtiofs daemon executable path or wrapper script.

args.description: Arguments for the virtiofs daemon command.

type.description: Mount kind; must be virtiofs for this entry.

tag.description: Stable QEMU mount tag or device identifier.

source.description: Host path or image path backing this mount.

read_only.description: Attach the mount or image read-only.

target.description: Optional guest mount target used for hotplug guest mount commands.

virtiofs.description: Virtiofs daemon socket and command settings.

type.description: Mount kind; must be 9p for this entry.

tag.description: Stable QEMU mount tag or device identifier.

source.description: Host path or image path backing this mount.

read_only.description: Attach the mount or image read-only.

size.description: Image size in MiB when creating the image.

fs.description: Filesystem type used when creating the image.

format.description: QEMU image format such as raw or qcow2.

create.description: Create and format the image if it does not exist.

label.description: Optional filesystem label used when creating the image.

direct.description: Use direct I/O cache settings for the block device.

serial.description: Optional disk serial exposed to the guest.

type.description: Mount kind; must be image for this entry.

image.description: Disk image creation and format settings.

mounts.description: Mount devices available for later hotplug attach or detach.

networks.description: Network devices available for later hotplug attach or detach.

path.description: Path to the guest kernel image.

initrd_path.description: Path to the guest initrd image.

params.description: Additional kernel command-line parameters appended after virtie defaults.

serial.description: Serial console mode: off print or console.

type.description: QEMU machine type to use when resolving device transports.

vcpu.description: Number of virtual CPUs to expose to the guest.

id.description: Optional machine identifier passed through to QEMU.

kvm.description: Whether QEMU should enable KVM acceleration when supported.

forward.description: Port forwarding rules for this network backend.

exec.description: Host command template invoked for enabled notification states.

states.description: Notification state names that should trigger the command.

exec.description: Command template used to launch QEMU; the first element is the QEMU binary.

fwd_tunnel_exec.description: Command template QEMU uses to forward host or guest ports.

user.description: Host user used for QEMU-related process policy when supported.

machine_options.description: Additional QEMU machine options merged into the resolved machine option list.

qmp_socket.description: Path to the QEMU Machine Protocol socket relative to the runtime state directory unless absolute.

guest_agent_socket.description: Path to the QEMU guest agent socket relative to the runtime state directory unless absolute.

exec.description: Host command template to start before QEMU.

vars.description: Template variables made available to this run command.

exec.description: SSH command template used to attach to the guest.

ready_socket.description: Guest readiness socket path relative to the runtime state directory unless absolute.

retry_delay.description: Seconds to wait between SSH readiness or connection retry attempts.

autoprovision.description: Automatically provision an SSH key after authentication failure.

cid_range.description: Inclusive range of vsock CIDs virtie may allocate at launch.

guest_dir.description: Guest workspace directory used in templates.

host_dir.description: Host workspace directory used in templates.

mount_cwd.description: Mount the current working directory into the guest after launch.

chown.description: Optional guest owner and group in owner:group form.

text.description: Inline text content to write to the guest path.

follow_links.description: Follow symlinks when reading source content from the host.

write_back.description: Copy guest changes back to the host source path during teardown.

source.description: Host source path for file content.

host_name.description: Guest-visible VM name used for QEMU naming and derived runtime files.

working_dir.description: Host working directory used to resolve relative paths in the manifest.

state_dir.description: Host directory used for runtime state such as locks sockets and generated files.

host.description: Host platform facts used while resolving QEMU defaults.

qemu.description: QEMU executable and host-side socket settings.

machine.description: Virtual machine type CPU memory and acceleration settings.

kernel.description: Guest kernel initrd and kernel command-line settings.

socket.description: Virtiofs daemon socket path relative to the runtime state directory unless absolute.

bin.description: Virtiofs daemon executable path or wrapper script.

args.description: Arguments for the virtiofs daemon command.

type.description: Mount kind; must be virtiofs for this entry.

tag.description: Stable QEMU mount tag or device identifier.

source.description: Host path or image path backing this mount.

read_only.description: Attach the mount or image read-only.

target.description: Optional guest mount target used for hotplug guest mount commands.

virtiofs.description: Virtiofs daemon socket and command settings.

type.description: Mount kind; must be 9p for this entry.

tag.description: Stable QEMU mount tag or device identifier.

source.description: Host path or image path backing this mount.

read_only.description: Attach the mount or image read-only.

size.description: Image size in MiB when creating the image.

fs.description: Filesystem type used when creating the image.

format.description: QEMU image format such as raw or qcow2.

create.description: Create and format the image if it does not exist.

label.description: Optional filesystem label used when creating the image.

direct.description: Use direct I/O cache settings for the block device.

serial.description: Optional disk serial exposed to the guest.

type.description: Mount kind; must be image for this entry.

image.description: Disk image creation and format settings.

mounts.description: Storage and filesystem devices attached at launch.

workspace.description: Workspace directories made available to guest-side templates and helpers.

networks.description: Network devices and port forwards attached at launch.

balloon.description: Virtio memory balloon device and optional controller settings.

ssh.description: SSH command and readiness settings for attaching to the guest.

vsock.description: Allowed runtime vsock CID allocation range.

write_files.description: Files copied into or synchronized with the guest through qemu guest agent.

notifications.description: Host command hooks invoked for selected runtime notification states.

run.description: Host-side processes started before QEMU and stopped during teardown.

hotplug.description: Devices that may be attached or detached after launch.

title: Virtie manifest
description: JSON Schema for the virtie manifest input format emitted by agentspace.
